Improper input validation vulnerability in AR Emoji Editor prior to version 4.4.03.5 in Android Q(10.0) and above allows untrusted applications to access arbitrary files with an escalated privilege.

Improper neutralization of user input in GitLab CE/EE versions 14.3 to 14.3.6, 14.4 to 14.4.4, and 14.5 to 14.5.2 allowed an attacker to exploit XSS by abusing the generation of the HTML code related
This affects the package node-ipc from 10.1.1 and before 10.1.3. This package contains malicious code, that targets users with IP located in Russia or Belarus, and overwrites their files with a heart
Improper caller check in AR Emoji prior to SMR Jun-2022 Release 1 allows untrusted applications to use some camera functions via deeplink.

An improper authorization issue in GitLab CE/EE affecting all versions from 15.0 prior to 15.3.5, 15.4 prior to 15.4.4, and 15.5 prior to 15.5.2 allows a malicious users to set emojis on internal note

The Denosaurs emoji package provides emojis for dinosaurs. Starting in version 0.1.0 and prior to version 0.3.0, the reTrimSpace regex has 2nd degree polynomial inefficiency, leading to a delayed resp

A NoSQL injection vulnerability has been identified in the listEmojiCustom method call within Rocket.Chat. This can be exploited by unauthenticated users when there is at least one custom emoji upload
An issue found in Wave Animated Keyboard Emoji v.1.70.7 for Android allows a local attacker to cause a denial of service via the database files.
An issue found in Wave Animated Keyboard Emoji v.1.70.7 for Android allows a local attacker to cause code execution and escalation of Privileges via the database files.
An issue found in Facemoji Emoji Keyboard v.2.9.1.2 for Android allows unauthorized apps to cause escalation of privilege attacks by manipulating the component.
An issue found in Facemoji Emoji Keyboard v.2.9.1.2 for Android allows a local attacker to cause a denial of service via the SharedPreference files.
